venue_summary: St Mary's Battersea, Battersea Barge, Clapham Grand, Arches Lane
description: Ten days of completely free jazz across multiple Battersea venues. No booking required. Opens with a jazz ballet — Hugo Jennings Big Band performing Prokofiev's Romeo and Juliet at Clapham Grand. Includes Scott Hamilton & Nat Steele, Deschanel Gordon Trio, Dave O'Higgins closing party.

venue_summary: Southbank Centre — Royal Festival Hall, Queen Elizabeth Hall, Riverside Terrace
description: Harry Styles curates Meltdown 2026. Jazz highlights: Kamasi Washington plays two shows (3pm tribute to Coltrane & Miles Davis, 8pm Fearless Movement Live), plus Steam Down free outdoor terrace takeover on 14 June.

venue_summary: Barbican, Southbank, Ronnie Scott's, 606, Wigmore Hall + 60 venues across London
description: The UK's biggest jazz festival — 10 days, 300+ events across 60+ London venues. 2026 highlights include Joe Lovano's Coltrane 100 project and Anouar Brahem. Also includes a free FreeStage at the Barbican.

venue_summary: Various venues across De Beauvoir Town, Hackney & Islington
description: Weekend-long jazz festival taking place across De Beauvoir Town, Hackney and Islington in July 2026.

venue_summary: Barbican, Cadogan Hall, Union Chapel, Theatre Royal Drury Lane, Royal Albert Hall
description: The EFG London Jazz Festival team's summer warm-up — global jazz stars across London's best venues. 2026 includes Pat Metheny (three shows at Barbican), Al Di Meola, Roberto Fonseca and more.
Free jazz stage at the High Tide Festival in Twickenham. Artists include Denys Baptiste/Tony Kofi Quintet, Misha Mullov-Abbado Sextet, GAB Trio. 1pm–7:30pm.
